originalTitle: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show
summary: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released minutes from its June 16-17 meeting.
content:
Federal Reserve officials were split last month about the future of interest rates, with policymakers entertaining scenarios in either direction, according to meeting minutes released Wednesday. In Kevin Warsh 's first meeting June 16-17 as chairman of the Federal Open Market Committee, participants saw outcomes where inflation could ease and allow lower rates, while others envisioned a scenario where price increases stay elevated and lead to hikes. During his post-meeting news conference, Warsh billed the debate as a "family fight" that ended with the committee unanimously voting to keep the Fed's benchmark funds rate anchored in a range between 3.5%-3.75%, where it has been for all of 2026. However, the minutes did not elaborate on any drama that had taken place and outlined divergent views from members without a bias to which way the committee was leaning. The dot-plot grid of individual members' expectations, in which Warsh did not participate, narrowly tilted toward one rate hike this year, then a cut in each of the following two years. Asked to judge their most likely scenario, "many participants indicated that the appropriate level of the federal funds rate would be within or slightly below the current target range at the end of this year," the minutes stated.

At the same time, the document also noted that "many other participants, however, assessed that the appropriate level of the federal funds rate would be above the current target range at the end of this year." "Participants noted that their future policy actions would depend on incoming information," the minutes said.

Inflation has been on the rise for much of the past year, fueled earlier by President Donald Trump's tariffs then exacerbated by the Iran war. Economists, though, have been split as to its durability, particularly since energy prices have plunged in recent weeks.

FOMC officials expressed "that inflation would remain elevated in the near term and then begin to decline as the effects of tariffs and energy price increases wane and other supply disruptions related to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz diminish. Participants judged that the risks to the inflation outlook were still tilted to the upside."

Participants also noted the impact of artificial intelligence, observing that the "ongoing strong demand for AI infrastructure would likely sustain upward pressure on prices for technology products and electricity." Warsh has stated he believes AI ultimately will be disinflationary due to productivity gains.

Markets reacted little to the minutes release, with stock market futures holding negative and Treasury yields rising.

"There's some ambiguity in the minutes, suggesting several competing views on policy," wrote Jeffrey Roach, chief economist at LPL Financial. "If we can tease out any forward guidance from the minutes, it would be the committee is working through a wide range of scenarios and will not commit to a specific scenario until the incoming data provides necessary clarity." The meeting summary, which at 14 pages was somewhat shorter though not dramatically so than the typical release, followed Warsh's repeated statements that Fed officials should communicate less about their future intentions. Keeping with that, the post-meeting statement was about one-third the size typical of the communique. Officials at the meeting seemed to approve of the tighter message. "A number of participants noted that it was an opportune time to consider significant changes to the FOMC's postmeeting statement," the minutes said. "A majority of participants remarked that they saw advantages in shortening the statement." The document otherwise provided broad strokes of what happened during the two-day session in which the Federal Open Market Committee approved the terse statement saying it was keeping its benchmark interest rate unchanged and was resolved to restore "price stability" to the U.S. economy.

Notably, it removed language that had indicated a prior easing bias, as "most participants emphasized that they preferred not to repeat the Language." The post-meeting statement eliminated boilerplate language to describe economic conditions and the committee's approach to achieving its twin goals of low inflation and full employment. The minutes come less than two months into Warsh's term as chairman, a position to which he was nominated by Trump . For years. the president had criticized Warsh's predecessor, Jerome Powell, for not pushing interest rates lower. Since taking the reins, Warsh has pledged to revamp the Fed's operations in a variety of manners. At the June news conference, he outlined five task forces that will address individual topics, including communication. The minutes simply stated the creation of the groups, noting that only "some participants commented that they welcomed the opportunity to review the Committee's communications tools and practices." Since then, Warsh had made only one public appearance. At a European Central Bank forum in Portugal, the central bank leader was largely circumspect about where he thinks policy should go, consistent with his distaste for so-called forward guidance on monetary policy intentions.
chineseTitle: 会议记录显示，美联储官员在上次会议上对利率方向存在分歧
chineseSummary: 美联储周三发布了 6 月 16 日至 17 日会议纪要。
chineseContent:
根据周三发布的会议纪要，上个月美联储官员对利率的未来存在分歧，政策制定者考虑了任一方向的情况。在凯文·沃什 (Kevin Warsh) 作为联邦公开市场委员会主席于 6 月 16 日至 17 日举行的首次会议上，与会者看到了通胀可能缓解并允许较低利率的结果，而其他人则设想了价格上涨保持在高位并导致加息的情况。在会后新闻发布会上，沃什将这场辩论描述为一场“家庭斗争”，最终委员会一致投票决定将美联储基准基金利率维持在 3.5%-3.75% 的范围内，2026 年全年都是如此。然而，会议纪要没有详细说明发生的任何戏剧性事件，并概述了成员们的不同观点，但没有偏见委员会的倾向。沃什没有参与的个人成员预期点阵图略微倾向于今年加息一次，然后在接下来的两年中每年降息。会议记录称，当被问及判断他们最可能出现的情况时，“许多与会者表示，今年年底联邦基金利率的适当水平将在当前目标范围内或略低于当前目标范围”。

同时，该文件还指出，“然而，许多其他参与者评估认为，今年年底联邦基金利率的适当水平将高于当前目标范围。”会议纪要称：“与会者指出，他们未来的政策行动将取决于收到的信息。”

过去一年的大部分时间里，通货膨胀一直在上升，早些时候受到唐纳德·特朗普总统的关税推动，随后又因伊朗战争而加剧。不过，经济学家对其持久性的看法存在分歧，特别是在最近几周能源价格暴跌之后。

FOMC官员表示，“通胀在短期内将保持在高位，然后随着关税和能源价格上涨的影响减弱以及与霍尔木兹海峡关闭相关的其他供应中断的影响减弱而开始下降。与会者判断通胀前景的风险仍然倾向于上行。”

与会者还注意到人工智能的影响，并指出“对人工智能基础设施的持续强劲需求可能会持续对技术产品和电力的价格造成上行压力。”沃什表示，他相信人工智能最终将因生产率的提高而抑制通货膨胀。

市场对会议纪要的发布反应不大，股市期货持负，国债收益率上升。

LPL Financial 首席经济学家杰弗里·罗奇 (Jeffrey Roach) 写道：“会议纪要中存在一些含糊之处，暗示了对政策的几种相互竞争的观点。” “如果我们能从会议纪要中梳理出任何前瞻性指导，那么委员会正在研究各种不同的情况，并且在收到的数据提供必要的清晰度之前不会承诺特定的情况。”会议纪要只有 14 页，比典型的新闻稿要短一些，但并不显着，沃什一再声明美联储官员应该减少对未来意图的沟通。与此保持一致的是，会后声明的篇幅约为典型公报的三分之一。与会官员似乎赞同更严格的信息。会议记录称，“许多与会者指出，现在是考虑对 FOMC 会后声明进行重大修改的最佳时机。” “大多数参与者表示，他们看到了缩短声明的优势。”该文件还概述了为期两天的会议期间发生的情况，联邦公开市场委员会在会议上批准了一份简短的声明，称其将维持基准利率不变，并决心恢复美国经济的“价格稳定”。

值得注意的是，它删除了表明先前宽松偏见的语言，因为“大多数参与者强调他们宁愿不重复该语言”。会后声明消除了描述经济状况和委员会实现低通胀和充分就业双重目标的方法的样板语言。这份会议纪要发布于沃什担任主席不到两个月的时间，他是由特朗普提名的。多年来。总统批评沃什的前任杰罗姆·鲍威尔没有降低利率。自上任以来，沃什承诺以多种方式改革美联储的运作。在六月的新闻发布会上，他概述了五个工作组，这些工作组将解决包括沟通在内的各个主题。会议记录只是简单地说明了这些小组的成立，并指出只有“一些参与者表示他们欢迎有机会审查委员会的沟通工具和做法”。从那时起，沃什只公开露面过一次。在葡萄牙举行的欧洲央行论坛上，这位央行领导人对于他认为政策应该走向的方向基本上持谨慎态度，这与他对所谓货币政策意图前瞻性指引的厌恶是一致的。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
